description: Full field specification for e-commerce product scraping with priorities

fields.name: competitor_name
fields.notes: Hardcoded. Example: `Supermercado Real - Paraguay`. Refer from the site

fields.name: competitor_type
fields.notes: Hardcoded. Example: `local_store` for DLOCAL or `dmart` for DMART

fields.name: store_name
fields.notes: Usually hardcoded or nil

fields.name: store_id
fields.notes: Usually hardcoded or nil

fields.name: country_iso
fields.notes: INFER - infer from the site. 2-letter code

fields.name: language
fields.notes: INFER - infer from the site. 3-letter code

fields.name: currency_code_lc
fields.notes: INFER - infer from the site/country. 3-letter code

fields.name: scraped_at_timestamp
fields.notes: Set directly with `Time.parse(page['fetched_at']).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),`

fields.name: competitor_product_id
fields.notes: FIND - can be from internal product id, SKU, barcode, etc. Anything that can be good identifier

fields.name: name
fields.notes: FIND - Product name - required field

fields.name: brand
fields.notes: FIND

fields.name: category_id
fields.notes: FIND or REFER - The category_id of main category. Probably hides inside html. Probably in listings (if exists, as fallback) or details page (prioritize from details page)

fields.name: category
fields.notes: FIND - probably exist in breadcrumb or find from product cards (just keep the main categories)

fields.name: sub_category
fields.notes: FIND - probably exist in breadcrumb or find from product cards. Format: [cat level 2,3,4...] joined by ` > `

fields.name: customer_price_lc
fields.notes: FIND - current price

fields.name: base_price_lc
fields.notes: FIND - the price before discount

fields.name: has_discount
fields.notes: DETERMINE - if customer_price_lc < base_price_lc then its TRUE and if customer_price_lc >= base_price_lc then its FALSE

fields.name: discount_percentage
fields.notes: DETERMINE - [(base_price_lc - customer_price_lc ) / base_price_lc]*100 rounded to max 7 decimal places as it is a float number

fields.name: rank_in_listing
fields.notes: FIND - from listings, usually referred from vars

fields.name: page_number
fields.notes: FIND - from listings, usually referred from vars

fields.name: product_pieces
fields.notes: null - extracted from name using MeasurementExtractor

fields.name: size_std
fields.notes: null - extracted from name using MeasurementExtractor

fields.name: size_unit_std
fields.notes: null - extracted from name using MeasurementExtractor

fields.name: description
fields.notes: FIND

fields.name: img_url
fields.notes: FIND - if multiple images persists, then create img_url_2 to 4 only

fields.name: barcode
fields.notes: FIND - if any. Type is 'int into str': the value is numeric but must be stored as a string. Example: barcode = '8888030066041'

fields.name: sku
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: url
fields.notes: from `page` variable:page['url']

fields.name: is_available
fields.notes: FIND

fields.name: crawled_source
fields.notes: HARDCODED: 'WEB' for website scrapers, 'APP' for mobile app scrapers. Use 'WEB' unless the scraper targets a mobile app API.

fields.name: is_promoted
fields.notes: DETERMINE - true for ANY form of promotion: banner, swimlane, top-of-page placement, bigger picture, mix-match, tiered pricing, OR strikethrough discount. Default fallback: set equal to has_discount if no other promotion signal is detectable. Strikethrough discount is NOT the only trigger.
fields.computation: has_discount (minimum default — look for other promotion signals first)

fields.name: type_of_promotion
fields.notes: FIND - if any. Can be 'Banner', or 'Badge', etc.

fields.name: promo_attributes
fields.notes: FIND - if any. JSON string. Each string value is wrapped in single quotes. Multiple values comma-separated inside single quotes. Empty values use empty string not "''". Examples: {"promo_detail":"'Any 2 @ $9.95'","free_item":""} or {"promo_detail":"'10% off', 'BestSeller'"} or {"description":"'Spend $138, get $8 off', 'Buy 1 item @ $0.90 Off'"}

fields.name: is_private_label
fields.notes: If the brand is different from the site name, then it is private label. Might need regex.

fields.name: latitude
fields.notes: null

fields.name: longitude
fields.notes: null

fields.name: reviews
fields.notes: FIND - if site shows product reviews. JSON string. If the site has no reviews feature at all: nil. If the site has reviews but product shows none: {"num_total_reviews":null,"avg_rating":null}. If values present: {"num_total_reviews":32,"avg_rating":4.2}. Numbers are NOT wrapped in single quotes.

format_spec.when_nil: site has no reviews feature at all

fields.name: store_reviews
fields.notes: FIND - if site shows store/seller reviews (distinct from product reviews). Same JSON string format as reviews. If not available: nil.

format_spec.when_nil: site has no store reviews feature

fields.name: item_attributes
fields.notes: FIND - if any. JSON string. Captures product badges, dietary labels, tags, or differentiator attributes (vegan, halal, bio, fairtrade, glutenfree, etc.). Each string value wrapped in single quotes. Empty values use empty string not "''". Keys are category labels (dietary attributes, tags, item badge, cuisine, etc.).

fields.name: item_identifiers
fields.notes: INFER - build from available identifiers (barcode, EAN, GTIN, UPC). JSON string. Each identifier value wrapped in single quotes. Multiple values for same key comma-separated inside single quotes. Empty values use empty string not "''".

fields.name: country_of_origin
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: variants
fields.notes: FIND - if any. Captures product option modifiers/variants (size, color, etc.). Unlike other struct fields, capture the raw string value as-is from the source — do NOT apply single-quote wrapping. Only applicable when the source exposes variant data. nil if not available.

fields.name: allergens
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: nutrition_facts
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: ingredients
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: dimensions
fields.notes: null

fields.name: img_url_2
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: img_url_3
fields.notes: FIND - if any

fields.name: img_url_4
fields.notes: FIND - if any
